example_prompt: Generate a PayGate hosted payment page URL and redirect my customer to it instead of pushing a USSD directly.

token.description: Merchant API key (Clé API) from the PayGate dashboard. Sent as a query string parameter.

amount.description: Transaction amount as an integer in the default currency (FCFA / XOF). No decimals.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the transaction.

identifier.description: Your own unique reference for this transaction (e.g. an order ID). Must be unique.

url.description: Callback URL where PayGate redirects the customer after the payment attempt.

phone.description: Optional. Pre-fills the customer phone number on the hosted page.

network.description: Optional. Pre-selects the network on the hosted page. MOOV = Moov / Flooz. TOGOCEL = Togocom / T-Money. If omitted, the customer chooses manually. Note: these values differ from the FLOOZ/TMONEY enum used by /v1/pay and /v1/disburse.

response_schema.description: GET /v1/page returns the hosted payment HTML page itself — there is no JSON response. Use this capability to build the redirect URL and send the user there.

redirect_url.description: The fully-built URL to redirect the customer to. Constructed locally from the query parameters.

error_schema.description: Errors are displayed on the hosted page itself. There is no structured error response.

gotchas: This is NOT a JSON API — it's a hosted page. The 'response' is an HTML page rendered to the customer's browser. The capability just builds the redirect URL.

The network enum is DIFFERENT from /v1/pay and /v1/disburse: here it's 'MOOV' or 'TOGOCEL'. On /v1/pay and /v1/disburse the same concept is encoded as 'FLOOZ' / 'TMONEY'. Do not pass FLOOZ or TMONEY to /v1/page — they are silently ignored.

After the customer completes (or abandons) the payment, PayGate redirects to the url query parameter. The redirect URL alone is NOT proof of payment — always re-verify server-side with check_payment_status before fulfilling.

If you also configured a webhook in your dashboard, you will receive the webhook on success regardless of whether the customer made it back to the redirect URL. The webhook is the more reliable signal.
Use this capability instead of initiate_payment when you want to let the customer choose their operator on a PayGate-hosted page rather than knowing the network upfront.

[MUST TELL USER] PayGate API documentation is private and only visible from inside the merchant dashboard. The query parameters and behavior of /v1/page may evolve — cross-check with the current docs in your account.
